title: erophaca baetica subsp. orientalis
intro: Erophaca baetica subsp. orientalis, also known as the oriental eryngo or Erophaca orientalis, is a perennial plant in the family Boraginaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have health benefits, such as aiding in digestion and reducing symptoms of respiratory issues, and has been used to treat conditions such as bronchitis and coughs. Erophaca baetica subsp. orientalis can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in some traditional remedies as a tincture or poultice.
